brief_title: Palbociclib Plus Letrozole in HR+/HER2- Advanced Breast Cancer
official_title: A Phase 2, Randomized, Double-Blind Study of Palbociclib Combined with Letrozole Versus Letrozole Alone as First-Line Treatment in Postmenopausal Women with HR-Positive, HER2-Negative Advanced Breast Cancer
interventions: Palbociclib 125mg oral capsule daily (21 days on, 7 days off) plus Letrozole 2.5mg daily
brief_summary: This Phase 2 study evaluates palbociclib in combination with letrozole versus letrozole alone as first-line therapy for postmenopausal women with hormone receptor-positive, HER2-negative advanced breast cancer. The primary endpoint is progression-free survival.
raw_inclusion_text:
1. Postmenopausal women aged 18 years or older

2. Histologically confirmed HR-positive (ER+ and/or PR+) breast cancer

3. Confirmed HER2-negative status (IHC 0/1+ or ISH non-amplified)

4. Locally advanced (not amenable to curative therapy) or metastatic disease

5. No prior systemic therapy for advanced/metastatic breast cancer

6. ECOG Performance Status 0-2

7. At least one measurable or evaluable lesion

8. Adequate bone marrow function (ANC >= 1000/μL, platelets >= 75,000/μL)

9. Adequate hepatic function

10. Adequate renal function (creatinine clearance >= 30 mL/min)

11. QTcF < 480 ms on screening ECG

12. Written informed consent
raw_exclusion_text:
1. HER2-positive breast cancer (IHC 3+ or ISH-amplified)

2. Prior CDK4/6 inhibitor therapy

3. Known CNS metastases unless previously treated and clinically stable

4. Concurrent use of strong CYP3A4 inhibitors

5. History of second malignancy within past 5 years (except adequately treated basal cell carcinoma)

6. Major surgery within 2 weeks of randomization

7. Inability to swallow oral medications

8. Known HIV infection or active viral hepatitis

inclusion_criteria.text: Postmenopausal women aged 18 years or older
Histologically confirmed HR-positive (ER+ and/or PR+) breast cancer
Confirmed HER2-negative status (IHC 0/1+ or ISH non-amplified)
Locally advanced (not amenable to curative therapy) or metastatic disease
No prior systemic therapy for advanced/metastatic breast cancer
At least one measurable or evaluable lesion

exclusion_criteria.text: Known CNS metastases unless previously treated and clinically stable
Concurrent use of strong CYP3A4 inhibitors
History of second malignancy within past 5 years (except adequately treated basal cell carcinoma)
Major surgery within 2 weeks of randomization
Known HIV infection or active viral hepatitis
